top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 106, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
77ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Bento Gonçalves' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
At GFNY Bento Gonçalves, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
GFNY Bento Gonçalves' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 106 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Bento Gonçalves. Train it until it feels boring.
8,202ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 106 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
8,202ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
106 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,202ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Bento Gonçalves, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: September in Bento Gonçalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before GFNY Bento Gonçalves. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Bento Gonçalves’s 106 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GFNY Bento Gonçalves. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Bento Gonçalves requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 106 miles of mixed terrain in Bento Gonçalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil.
